description: Give side panel tool icons a proper foreground color when enabled, and grey them out when disabled — replacing the current always-grey appearance.

userStories.title: Color tool icons based on enabled state
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want enabled tool icons to have a prominent foreground color so that I can instantly distinguish enabled tools from disabled ones without looking at the switch toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolIcon accepts an `enabled` prop (boolean, defaults to true)
When enabled=true: the icon uses `text-foreground` (black in light mode, near-white in dark) and the container uses `bg-muted/50 border-border` (current container style preserved)
When enabled=false: the icon uses `text-muted-foreground` (the current always-grey color) and the container uses `bg-muted/30 border-border/60` to subtly fade the entire icon block
The transition between states is smooth — add `transition-colors` to both the container div and the icon element
ToolRow passes its existing `enabled` prop through to ToolIcon
Both PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard tool rows reflect the new visual distinction without any changes to those files (they already pass `enabled` to ToolRow)
The FallbackIcon (Wrench) also respects the enabled prop with the same color logic
userStories.notes:
The change is confined to two files:

1. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolIcon.tsx` (26 lines total):
   - Add `enabled?: boolean` to `ToolIconProps` (default `true`)
   - Change the icon className from hardcoded `text-muted-foreground` to a conditional: `enabled ? 'text-foreground' : 'text-muted-foreground'`
   - Change the container className: when disabled, use `bg-muted/30 border-border/60` instead of `bg-muted/50 border-border` to subtly fade the box too
   - Add `transition-colors` to the container div's className
   - The FallbackIcon currently hardcodes `text-muted-foreground` — it needs to become a function of `enabled` too. One approach: make FallbackIcon accept `enabled` as a prop, or inline it.
   - Use the `cn()` utility from `../lib/cn.js` for clean conditional class merging

2. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx` (46 lines total):
   - Pass the existing `enabled` prop to `<ToolIcon icon={icon} enabled={enabled} />` (line 25)
   - No other changes needed — ToolRow already receives `enabled` from its parent

Design rationale: `text-foreground` is the standard high-contrast text color in the theme (#000 light / #f5f5f5 dark). Using it for enabled icons makes them feel alive and intentional — part of the active UI. `text-muted-foreground` (#5a5a5a light / #a0a0a0 dark) is already the muted/disabled convention. The container border/bg fade is subtle but reinforces the distinction.

Do NOT use any hardcoded hex colors or Tailwind default palette colors — only theme tokens. Do NOT add new CSS variables to styles.css — the existing tokens are sufficient.
